info.title: SEPM POLICIES API
info.description: Self-contained OpenAPI 2.0 specification for SEPM policies endpoints.

company.description: Optional attribute identifying the process.  The name of the company that published the object.

directory.description: Optional attribute identifying the process.  The directory where the object was originally found.

name.description: Optional attribute identifying the process.  The name of the file object; for example, iexplore.exe

sha2.description: The SHA-256, SHA-1, or MD5 checksum of the object content.

size.description: Optional attribute identifying the process.  The size of the object, in bytes; for example, 69472.

algorithm.description: The algorithm used to create the fingerprint.  SHA-1 support only for SAEPv1 for certificate thumbprint.

value.description: The hash of the object content.

directories.description: Directories on Linux platforms at the exceptions policy

extension_list.description: Extension on Linux platforms at the exceptions policy

files.description: Files on mac platforms at the exceptions policy

action.description: The action of the threat in exceptions policy.

deleted.description: Note if the rule need be deleted when we PATCH a request

action.description: Action to take on the process

processfile.description: The process to block, delete or quarantine

deleted.description: Note if the rule need be deleted when we PATCH a request

signature_company_name.description: The company name on the certificate

signature_fingerprints.description: List of hash of the digital certificates [Only supported with V2 APIs]. If SHA1 is provided here, `signature_fingerprint`'s value will be ignored

signature_issuer.description: he issuer of the object signature

deleted.description: Note if the rule need be deleted when we PATCH a request

pathvariable.description: A prefix variable indicates a well-known Windows folder

recursive.description: Indicates whether sub-folders are also excluded

scantype.description: Type of scan that exclude the folder

deleted.description: Note if the rule need be deleted when we PATCH a request

processfile.description: The process to block or prompt

deleted.description: Note if the rule need be deleted when we PATCH a request

domain.description: The URL domain extracted from the URL; for example, www.files.com or an ip address.  Domain names can include wildcards.

deleted.description: Note if the rule need be deleted when we PATCH a request

applicationcontrol.description: Exclude this file from application control

deleted.description: Note if the rule need be deleted when we PATCH a request

path.description: The path to the object.  Relative path if pathvariable is defined.  Full path if pathvariable is [NONE] or not specified

pathvariable.description: A prefix variable indicates a well-known Windows folder

securityrisk.description: Exclude this file from security risk

recursive.description: Indicates whether sub-folders are also excluded

path.description: The path to the object.  Relative path if pathvariable is defined.  Full path if pathvariable is [NONE] or not specified

pathvariable.description: A prefix variable indicates a well-known Mac folder

deleted.description: Note if the rule need be deleted when we PATCH a request

enabled.description: Ability to disable a rule temporarily from enforcement without deleting it

source.description: Which 3rd-party management system was the source of the rule; ex, SEP, ATP

delete_unused_content_after.description: Time of deleting unused content after it

hosts.description: The hosts of the host group policy component. The host is any of Mac, IpV4, IpV6, IpV4Range, IpV6Range, DnsName, DnsHost, IpV4Subnet, IpV6Subnet

name.description: Name of host group policy component.

enabled.description: Ability to disable a rule temporarily from enforcement without deleting it

source.description: Which 3rd-party management system was the source of the rule; ex, SEP, ATP

linux.description: Linux setting configuration in LiveUpdate setting policy

mac.description: MAC setting configuration in LiveUpdate setting policy

windows.description: windows setting configuration in LiveUpdate setting policy

av_def_age.description: AV definition age of the schedule

av_def_check_enabled.description: Enable AV definition check of the schedule

av_def_check_freq.description: AV definition check frequency of the schedule

disconnection_check_duration.description: Disconnection check duration of the schedule

disconnection_check_enabled.description: Enable disconnection check of the schedule

disconnection_check_freq.description: Disconnection check frequency of the schedule

use_default_mgmt_server.description: If uses default management server as LiveUpdate server

customrules.description: Administrator defined application to protect. If an application is listed here without techniques, it is added to the coverage using the global technique state.

defaultruleoverrides.description: Overrides to the default rules. If an application is listed here without technique, it is completely disabled.

enableadvanced.description: Enables the advanced policy in addition to the basic one for Memory Exploit Mitigation.

enabled.description: Enables the basic policy for Memory Exploit Mitigation.

globalauditmodeoverride.description: Put all techniques into audit mode, even future techniques

names.description: Rule set of the multiple GUP

path.description: The path of the process to protect. Wildcards are permitted.

techniqueoverrides.description: Technique override for a specific path.

action.description: The action to take when the rule is enabled.

id.description: The signature ID for the technique.

log_action.description: The log action to take when the rule is enabled.

name.description: The name of the threat, as identified by the specific definition engine.

state.description: Indicates whether the technique is enabled or disabled.

advanced.description: Advanced configuration in LiveUpdate setting policy

schedule.description: Download schedule configuration in LiveUpdate setting policy

server.description: Source server configuration in LiveUpdate setting policy

enabled.description: Indicates whether the policy is enabled or not.

sources.description: A list of data objects provided by the policy source, which includes the ID, the name and the version.

rep_discovered_days.description: Convict file if downloaded and has this first seen number of days or fewer

enabled.description: Indicates whether this rule is enabled

rep_prevalence_band.description: Convict file if downloaded and has this prevalence band or lower

exclusiveedit.description: Gets ExclusiveEdit flag provided by the Source

version.description: Gets Source version of the policy

release_delay_days.description: Number of days to wait to upgrade after SEPM publishes a release.

schedule.description: Permissible days and time on which to upgrade.

daily.description: The days to do the upgrade.

end_time.description: End time of window where upgrades are permitted. Format is 0000-00-00TThh:mm:ss.sTZD

retry_enabled.description: Enable retries if client missed upgrade.

time_window.description: Number of seconds in the distribution window. The client picks a random time within the window for its own upgrade.

time.description: Start time of window where upgrades are permitted. Format is 0000-00-00TThh:mm:ss.sTZD
